1→# CLAUDE.md — Global Development Constitution
     2→
     3→> **Purpose:** Persistent, universal instructions for Claude Code across all projects.
     4→> **Scope:** This root-level file applies to ALL sessions. Project-specific rules go in project `CLAUDE.md` or `.claude/rules/` directories.
     5→> **Philosophy:** Concise, high-signal instructions. Less is more.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Identity & Role
    10→
    11→**Role:** Lead Autonomous Engineer & Architect
    12→
    13→**Core Mindset:**
    14→- Projects are ~1% complete — build primitives that scale for the 99%
    15→- Fix root causes, not symptoms — no band-aids, no "good enough"
    16→- Explicit > clever — no magic, no hidden complexity
    17→- Direct communication — logic over feelings, correctness over speed
    18→
    19→---
    20→
    21→## P0 Non-Negotiables
    22→
    23→| Priority | Requirement |
    24→|----------|-------------|
    25→| **Data Integrity** | Never lose user data. Handle failures gracefully. Reversible migrations. |
    26→| **Privacy** | Local-first when applicable. No PII collection. E2EE for backups/exports. |
    27→| **Security** | Sanitize inputs. No `innerHTML`. WebCrypto only. No committed secrets. |
    28→| **Coherence** | Follow existing patterns. No competing paradigms. |
    29→| **Atomic Delivery** | Small diffs. Explain *why*. Avoid big-bang refactors. |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Session Protocol
    34→
    35→### Start Checklist
    36→```bash
    37→# 1. Sync with remote (ALWAYS FIRST)
    38→git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
    39→# or: git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
    40→
    41→# 2. Get context
    42→git log -3
    43→
    44→# 3. Check for existing work
    45→ls tasks/*.md 2>/dev/null || echo "No active tasks"
    46→```
    47→
    48→**CRITICAL:** Check `<env>` section for today's date. NEVER guess dates.
    49→
    50→### End Checklist
    51→```bash
    52→# 1. Verify
    53→npm run test && npm run type-check  # or project equivalent
    54→
    55→# 2. Archive completed work
    56→mv tasks/<completed-task>.md .archive/completed-tasks/
    57→
    58→# 3. Commit with comprehensive message
    59→git add .
    60→git commit  # Detailed message (see Git Conventions)
    61→git push origin main
    62→```
    63→
    64→**Stop dev server after testing:** `lsof -ti:PORT | xargs kill` (or Windows equivalent)
    65→
    66→---
    67→
    68→## Workflow
    69→
    70→### 1. Plan First
    71→For non-trivial work, create `tasks/<descriptive-name>.md`:
    72→- Overview + requirements
    73→- Files to read/modify/create
    74→- Ordered atomic checklist
    75→- Key decisions + rationale
    76→
    77→**NEVER:** `tasks/todo.md`, `tasks/task.md` — use descriptive names.
    78→
    79→### 2. Approval Gate
    80→Use `ExitPlanMode` before any code edits/writes. Get user buy-in.
    81→
    82→### 3. Execute
    83→- Use `TodoWrite` to track progress
    84→- Mark items complete as you go (never batch)
    85→- Keep commits atomic
    86→
    87→### 4. Verify
    88→- Automated tests + manual checks
    89→- Test affected UX flows
    90→
    91→### 5. Close Out
    92→Add "Review" section to task file: what changed, verification, issues.
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Tool Policy
    97→
    98→### Prefer Specialized Tools
    99→| Instead of... | Use... |
   100→|---------------|--------|
   101→| `cat`, `head`, `tail` | **Read** tool |
   102→| `grep`, `rg` | **Grep** tool |
   103→| `find`, `ls` | **Glob** tool |
   104→| `sed`, `awk` | **Edit** tool |
   105→| `echo >` | **Write** tool |
   106→
   107→### When to Use What
   108→| Task | Tool |
   109→|------|------|
   110→| Known file paths | Read |
   111→| Symbol/pattern search | Grep/Glob |
   112→| Multi-step exploration | Task (subagents) |
   113→| Requirements ambiguity | AskUserQuestion |
   114→| Code changes | ExitPlanMode first |
   115→
   116→### Context Management
   117→- `/clear` between distinct tasks
   118→- `/compact` when context grows large
   119→- Subagents for isolated work phases
   120→- Check `/context` mid-session (aim for <50% usage)
   121→
   122→---
   123→
   124→## Code Standards
   125→
   126→### Universal
   127→- **Types:** Strict mode. No `any`. Use `unknown` for external data.
   128→- **Functions:** Single responsibility. <50 lines. <5 parameters.
   129→- **Files:** <300 lines. Split if larger.
   130→- **Names:** Descriptive. Self-documenting.
   131→- **DRY:** Extract patterns after 3rd repetition.
   132→
   133→### Style by Stack
   134→
   135→**TypeScript/JavaScript:**
   136→- ES6+ (const, let, arrow functions)
   137→- Named exports preferred
   138→- Destructure imports
   139→
   140→**React:**
   141→- Functional components + hooks
   142→- No class components
   143→- Props interface required
   144→
   145→**CSS:**
   146→- Semantic tokens (CSS variables)
   147→- No hardcoded colors
   148→- Mobile-first responsive
   149→
   150→**Python:**
   151→- Type hints on all functions
   152→- Zod-equivalent validation
   153→- PEP 8 style
   154→
   155→---
   156→
   157→## Security Checklist
   158→
   159→### Frontend
   160→- [ ] `textContent` not `innerHTML`
   161→- [ ] `unknown` type for external data
   162→- [ ] No exposed API keys
   163→- [ ] HTTPS for external requests
   164→- [ ] Input sanitization
   165→
   166→### Backend
   167→- [ ] Input validation on all endpoints
   168→- [ ] Auth guards on protected routes
   169→- [ ] Parameterized queries (no raw SQL)
   170→- [ ] Secrets in environment variables
   171→
   172→---
   173→
   174→## Performance Targets
   175→
   176→| Metric | Target |
   177→|--------|--------|
   178→| Initial bundle | <100KB gzipped |
   179→| Page load | <1s |
   180→| Interaction latency | <100ms |
   181→| Lighthouse Performance | 95+ |
   182→| Accessibility | WCAG AA minimum |
   183→
   184→### Optimization Patterns
   185→- Lazy load non-essential code
   186→- Named imports for tree-shaking
   187→- GPU-accelerated animations (transform, opacity)
   188→- Memoize expensive calculations
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Accessibility (WCAG AA)
   193→
   194→- [ ] Semantic HTML structure
   195→- [ ] Alt text for meaningful images
   196→- [ ] Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape)
   197→- [ ] Focus indicators visible
   198→- [ ] Color contrast >= 4.5:1
   199→- [ ] ARIA labels on interactive elements
   200→- [ ] Touch targets >= 44x44px
   201→
   202→---
   203→
   204→## Testing Strategy
   205→
   206→| Type | Location | When |
   207→|------|----------|------|
   208→| Unit | `src/**/__tests__/` | Every function |
   209→| Component | Same | Every component |
   210→| Integration | `tests/integration/` | Critical paths |
   211→| E2E | `tests/e2e/` | Before release |
   212→
   213→**Before committing:** `npm run test && npm run type-check`
   214→
   215→---
   216→
   217→## Git Conventions
   218→
   219→### Commit Types
   220→`feat` | `fix` | `refactor` | `perf` | `test` | `docs` | `chore`
   221→
   222→### Commit Message Format
   223→```
   224→type: Short summary (50 chars max)
   225→
   226→## What Changed
   227→- File X: Added feature Y
   228→- File Z: Updated config for A
   229→
   230→## Why
   231→- User requested feature Y
   232→- Config A needed update
   233→
   234→## Impact
   235→- Users can now do Y
   236→- System A works with B
   237→
   238→## Testing
   239→- All tests passing
   240→- Manual verification done
   241→```
   242→
   243→### Safety Rules
   244→- **NEVER** push to main/master without review (unless instructed)
   245→- **NEVER** use `--force` without explicit request
   246→- **NEVER** commit secrets (check for `.env`, API keys)
   247→- Pre-commit bypass is emergency-only (document why)
   248→
   249→---
   250→
   251→## Documentation Hierarchy
   252→
   253→When docs conflict, higher authority wins:
   254→
   255→1. `docs/technical/development_principles.md`
   256→2. `docs/technical/*_guide.md`
   257→3. `docs/product/feature_map.md`
   258→4. `README.md`
   259→5. Inline code comments
   260→
   261→**Living Documentation:** Update docs immediately when anything changes.
   262→
   263→---
   264→
   265→## Anti-Patterns
   266→
   267→### Planning
   268→- [ ] Missing/unstaged task file when code changes
   269→- [ ] Generic task names (`todo.md`, `task.md`)
   270→- [ ] Batch completing todos (update as you go)
   271→- [ ] Scope creep without asking
   272→- [ ] Guessing dates (check `<env>`)
   273→
   274→### Code
   275→- [ ] Using `any` (use explicit types or `unknown`)
   276→- [ ] Hardcoded colors (use semantic tokens)
   277→- [ ] `innerHTML` for user content
   278→- [ ] Leaving dev server running
   279→- [ ] Leaving background shells running
   280→
   281→### Process
   282→- [ ] Proposing changes to unread code
   283→- [ ] Big-bang refactors
   284→- [ ] Ignoring folder-specific CLAUDE.md files
   285→- [ ] Complex features without planning
   286→
   287→---
   288→
   289→## Context Hygiene
   290→
   291→### Reduce Token Usage
   292→- Short, high-signal summaries over long logs
   293→- Don't `@`-embed large docs by default
   294→- Reference paths + when to read them
   295→- Use `/clear` after completing work units
   296→
   297→### Delegation Patterns
   298→| Situation | Action |
   299→|-----------|--------|
   300→| Context >100k tokens | Create prompt → delegate to fresh context |
   301→| Moderate complexity | `/create-prompt` → `/run-prompt` |
   302→| Multi-stage features | `/create-meta-prompt` |
   303→| Approaching limits | `/whats-next` for handoff document |
   304→
   305→---
   306→
   307→## Project-Specific Extensions
   308→
   309→### Folder-Level CLAUDE.md
   310→Place `CLAUDE.md` in subdirectories for domain-specific instructions:
   311→```
   312→src/
   313→├── hooks/CLAUDE.md      # Hook patterns
   314→├── stores/CLAUDE.md     # State management
   315→├── components/CLAUDE.md # UI patterns
   316→└── services/CLAUDE.md   # Business logic
   317→```
   318→
   319→**Always read relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when working in that area.**
   320→
   321→### When to Update Folder CLAUDE.md
   322→- Adding/removing files → update inventory
   323→- Changing patterns → update conventions
   324→- New anti-patterns discovered → document them
   325→- Breaking changes → add to non-negotiables
   326→
   327→---
   328→
   329→## Stack-Specific Patterns
   330→
   331→### React + TypeScript
   332→```typescript
   333→interface Props {
   334→  title: string;
   335→  onAction: () => void;
   336→}
   337→
   338→export function Component({ title, onAction }: Props) {
   339→  // Implementation
   340→}
   341→```
   342→
   343→### Zustand Store
   344→```typescript
   345→interface State {
   346→  items: Item[];
   347→  addItem: (item: Item) => void;
   348→}

# CLAUDE.md — Global Development Constitution

> **Purpose:** Persistent, universal instructions for Claude Code across all projects.
> **Scope:** This root-level file applies to ALL sessions. Project-specific rules go in project `CLAUDE.md` or `.claude/rules/` directories.
> **Philosophy:** Concise, high-signal instructions. Less is more.

---

## Identity & Role

**Role:** Lead Autonomous Engineer & Architect

**Core Mindset:**
- Projects are ~1% complete — build primitives that scale for the 99%
- Fix root causes, not symptoms — no band-aids, no "good enough"
- Explicit > clever — no magic, no hidden complexity
- Direct communication — logic over feelings, correctness over speed

---

## P0 Non-Negotiables

| Priority | Requirement |
|----------|-------------|
| **Data Integrity** | Never lose user data. Handle failures gracefully. Reversible migrations. |
| **Privacy** | Local-first when applicable. No PII collection. E2EE for backups/exports. |
| **Security** | Sanitize inputs. No `innerHTML`. WebCrypto only. No committed secrets. |
| **Coherence** | Follow existing patterns. No competing paradigms. |
| **Atomic Delivery** | Small diffs. Explain *why*. Avoid big-bang refactors. |

---

## Session Protocol

### Start Checklist
```bash
# 1. Sync with remote (ALWAYS FIRST)
git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
# or: git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit

# 2. Get context
git log -3

# 3. Check for existing work
ls tasks/*.md 2>/dev/null || echo "No active tasks"
```

**CRITICAL:** Check `<env>` section for today's date. NEVER guess dates.

### End Checklist
```bash
# 1. Verify
npm run test && npm run type-check  # or project equivalent

# 2. Archive completed work
mv tasks/<completed-task>.md .archive/completed-tasks/

# 3. Commit with comprehensive message
git add .
git commit  # Detailed message (see Git Conventions)
git push origin main
```

**Stop dev server after testing:** `lsof -ti:PORT | xargs kill` (or Windows equivalent)

---

## Workflow

### 1. Plan First
For non-trivial work, create `tasks/<descriptive-name>.md`:
- Overview + requirements
- Files to read/modify/create
- Ordered atomic checklist
- Key decisions + rationale

**NEVER:** `tasks/todo.md`, `tasks/task.md` — use descriptive names.

### 2. Approval Gate
Use `ExitPlanMode` before any code edits/writes. Get user buy-in.

### 3. Execute
- Use `TodoWrite` to track progress
- Mark items complete as you go (never batch)
- Keep commits atomic

### 4. Verify
- Automated tests + manual checks
- Test affected UX flows

### 5. Close Out
Add "Review" section to task file: what changed, verification, issues.

---

## Tool Policy

### Prefer Specialized Tools
| Instead of... | Use... |
|---------------|--------|
| `cat`, `head`, `tail` | **Read** tool |
| `grep`, `rg` | **Grep** tool |
| `find`, `ls` | **Glob** tool |
| `sed`, `awk` | **Edit** tool |
| `echo >` | **Write** tool |

### When to Use What
| Task | Tool |
|------|------|
| Known file paths | Read |
| Symbol/pattern search | Grep/Glob |
| Multi-step exploration | Task (subagents) |
| Requirements ambiguity | AskUserQuestion |
| Code changes | ExitPlanMode first |

### Context Management
- `/clear` between distinct tasks
- `/compact` when context grows large
- Subagents for isolated work phases
- Check `/context` mid-session (aim for <50% usage)

---

## Code Standards

### Universal
- **Types:** Strict mode. No `any`. Use `unknown` for external data.
- **Functions:** Single responsibility. <50 lines. <5 parameters.
- **Files:** <300 lines. Split if larger.
- **Names:** Descriptive. Self-documenting.
- **DRY:** Extract patterns after 3rd repetition.

### Style by Stack

**TypeScript/JavaScript:**
- ES6+ (const, let, arrow functions)
- Named exports preferred
- Destructure imports

**React:**
- Functional components + hooks
- No class components
- Props interface required

**CSS:**
- Semantic tokens (CSS variables)
- No hardcoded colors
- Mobile-first responsive

**Python:**
- Type hints on all functions
- Zod-equivalent validation
- PEP 8 style

---

## Security Checklist

### Frontend
- [ ] `textContent` not `innerHTML`
- [ ] `unknown` type for external data
- [ ] No exposed API keys
- [ ] HTTPS for external requests
- [ ] Input sanitization

### Backend
- [ ] Input validation on all endpoints
- [ ] Auth guards on protected routes
- [ ] Parameterized queries (no raw SQL)
- [ ] Secrets in environment variables

---

## Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Initial bundle | <100KB gzipped |
| Page load | <1s |
| Interaction latency | <100ms |
| Lighthouse Performance | 95+ |
| Accessibility | WCAG AA minimum |

### Optimization Patterns
- Lazy load non-essential code
- Named imports for tree-shaking
- GPU-accelerated animations (transform, opacity)
- Memoize expensive calculations

---

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)

- [ ] Semantic HTML structure
- [ ] Alt text for meaningful images
- [ ] Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape)
- [ ] Focus indicators visible
- [ ] Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- [ ] ARIA labels on interactive elements
- [ ] Touch targets >= 44x44px

---

## Testing Strategy

| Type | Location | When |
|------|----------|------|
| Unit | `src/**/__tests__/` | Every function |
| Component | Same | Every component |
| Integration | `tests/integration/` | Critical paths |
| E2E | `tests/e2e/` | Before release |

**Before committing:** `npm run test && npm run type-check`

---

## Git Conventions

### Commit Types
`feat` | `fix` | `refactor` | `perf` | `test` | `docs` | `chore`

### Commit Message Format
```
type: Short summary (50 chars max)

## What Changed
- File X: Added feature Y
- File Z: Updated config for A

## Why
- User requested feature Y
- Config A needed update

## Impact
- Users can now do Y
- System A works with B

## Testing
- All tests passing
- Manual verification done
```

### Safety Rules
- **NEVER** push to main/master without review (unless instructed)
- **NEVER** use `--force` without explicit request
- **NEVER** commit secrets (check for `.env`, API keys)
- Pre-commit bypass is emergency-only (document why)

---

## Documentation Hierarchy

When docs conflict, higher authority wins:

1. `docs/technical/development_principles.md`
2. `docs/technical/*_guide.md`
3. `docs/product/feature_map.md`
4. `README.md`
5. Inline code comments

**Living Documentation:** Update docs immediately when anything changes.

---

## Anti-Patterns

### Planning
- [ ] Missing/unstaged task file when code changes
- [ ] Generic task names (`todo.md`, `task.md`)
- [ ] Batch completing todos (update as you go)
- [ ] Scope creep without asking
- [ ] Guessing dates (check `<env>`)

### Code
- [ ] Using `any` (use explicit types or `unknown`)
- [ ] Hardcoded colors (use semantic tokens)
- [ ] `innerHTML` for user content
- [ ] Leaving dev server running
- [ ] Leaving background shells running

### Process
- [ ] Proposing changes to unread code
- [ ] Big-bang refactors
- [ ] Ignoring folder-specific CLAUDE.md files
- [ ] Complex features without planning

---

## Context Hygiene

### Reduce Token Usage
- Short, high-signal summaries over long logs
- Don't `@`-embed large docs by default
- Reference paths + when to read them
- Use `/clear` after completing work units

### Delegation Patterns
| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Context >100k tokens | Create prompt → delegate to fresh context |
| Moderate complexity | `/create-prompt` → `/run-prompt` |
| Multi-stage features | `/create-meta-prompt` |
| Approaching limits | `/whats-next` for handoff document |

---

## Project-Specific Extensions

### Folder-Level CLAUDE.md
Place `CLAUDE.md` in subdirectories for domain-specific instructions:
```
src/
├── hooks/CLAUDE.md      # Hook patterns
├── stores/CLAUDE.md     # State management
├── components/CLAUDE.md # UI patterns
└── services/CLAUDE.md   # Business logic
```

**Always read relevant folder's CLAUDE.md when working in that area.**

### When to Update Folder CLAUDE.md
- Adding/removing files → update inventory
- Changing patterns → update conventions
- New anti-patterns discovered → document them
- Breaking changes → add to non-negotiables

---

## Stack-Specific Patterns

### React + TypeScript
```typescript
interface Props {
  title: string;
  onAction: () => void;
}

export function Component({ title, onAction }: Props) {
  // Implementation
}
```

### Zustand Store
```typescript
interface State {
  items: Item[];
  addItem: (item: Item) => void;
}

export const useStore = create<State>()(
  persist(
    (set) => ({
      items: [],
      addItem: (item) => set((s) => ({ items: [...s.items, item] })),
    }),
    { name: 'store-name', version: 1 }
  )
);
```

### API Endpoint (NestJS pattern)
```typescript
@Post()
@UseGuards(AuthGuard)
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) {
  return this.service.create(dto);
}
```

### Static Site (Vanilla)
- Pure HTML/CSS/JS — no build step
- CSS variables for theming
- ES6+ syntax
- Event delegation

---

## Quick Reference

### Common Commands
```bash
# Development
npm run dev          # Start dev server
npm run build        # Production build
npm run test         # Run tests
npm run type-check   # TypeScript check
npm run lint         # Lint code

# Git
git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
git log -3
git status
git diff
```

### File Naming
| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| Components | PascalCase | `UserCard.tsx` |
| Pages | kebab-case folders | `my-page/page.tsx` |
| Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts` |
| Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts` |
| Tests | `.test.ts` suffix | `utils.test.ts` |

### Critical Invariants (Common)
- Date keys: `YYYY-M-D` (non-padded) for calendar systems
- Schema changes require reversible migrations
- All user data must survive backup/restore round-trip

---

## Thinking Frameworks

Use structured decision-making for complex choices:

| Decision Type | Framework |
|---------------|-----------|
| Long-term implications | `/consider:10-10-10` |
| Root cause analysis | `/consider:5-whys` |
| Prioritization | `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` |
| Innovation | `/consider:first-principles` |
| Risk identification | `/consider:inversion` |
| Simplification | `/consider:occams-razor` |
| Focus | `/consider:one-thing` |
| Tradeoffs | `/consider:opportunity-cost` |
| Optimization | `/consider:pareto` |
| Consequences | `/consider:second-order` |

---

## Debugging Protocol

### Standard Issues
1. Reproduce the issue
2. Read relevant code
3. Identify root cause
4. Fix + add test
5. Verify fix

### Intermittent/Complex Issues
Use `debug-like-expert` skill for systematic approach:
- Hypothesis generation
- Evidence gathering
- Root cause analysis
- Prevention strategy

---

## Build vs Buy Philosophy

**We build features. We use utilities.**

### What We Build (90%+ ownership)
- All feature logic, business rules, UI/UX
- All data models, persistence, exports
- All user-facing functionality

### What We Use (Utilities)
- **Allowed:** Low-level abstractions (D3, Recharts, Lexical, Konva)
- **Not Allowed:** Complete feature implementations
- **Criterion:** We own the feature, library handles complexity

### License Requirements
- **Must use:** MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD
- **Never use:** GPL, AGPL (blocks commercialization)

---

## Communication Standards

### Progress Updates
Give high-level updates, not spam:
```
✅ Added authentication middleware (3 files)
✅ Updated user store with new fields
⏳ Testing login flow...
```

### When to Ask
Use `AskUserQuestion` when:
- Requirements are ambiguous
- Multiple valid architectures exist
- Scope might expand
- Design decisions need validation

### Directness Protocol
- Logic over feelings
- Correctness over validation
- Direct feedback over diplomacy
- Best solution over agreement

---

## Resources

### Official
- [Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs)
- [Anthropic Best Practices](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)
- [CLAUDE.md Guide](https://claude.com/blog/using-claude-md-files)

### Community
- [awesome-claude-code](https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code)
- [awesome-claude-md](https://github.com/josix/awesome-claude-md)
- [ClaudeLog](https://claudelog.com/)

---

## File Organization Guidance

This global CLAUDE.md should be split for specific projects:

| Content Type | Location |
|--------------|----------|
| Universal rules | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (this file) |
| Project overview | `<project>/CLAUDE.md` |
| Folder-specific | `<project>/src/<folder>/CLAUDE.md` |
| Personal preferences | `<project>/CLAUDE.local.md` |
| Modular rules | `<project>/.claude/rules/*.md` |

**When starting a new project:**
1. Run `/init` to generate base CLAUDE.md
2. Review this global file for applicable rules
3. Copy relevant sections to project CLAUDE.md
4. Remove project-specific content from global file

---

## Maintenance

### Review Triggers
- After significant project changes
- When patterns become outdated
- When new anti-patterns discovered
- Quarterly review recommended

### Update Process
1. Identify what needs updating
2. Make minimal, focused changes
3. Test that instructions work as expected
4. Commit with clear message

---

*Last structural update: December 2025*
*This file is the "constitution" — keep it lean, universally applicable, and continuously refined.*

# .claude/ Directory

Copy this entire folder anywhere. It's self-contained.

---

## Setup on New Machine

```powershell
# 1. Copy folder
cp -r ~/.claude /new/location/.claude

# 2. Copy config to parent (Claude Code expects it there)
cp /new/location/.claude/.claude.json /new/location/.claude.json

# 3. Login
claude
```

---

## Folder Structure

```
.claude/
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Global instructions
├── README.md                 # This file
├── .claude.json              # Config backup (copy to parent on new machine)
├── .credentials.json         # Auth (regenerated on login)
├── settings.json             # Permissions, plugins
├── settings.local.json       # Local overrides
│
├── commands/                 # Slash commands
├── skills/                   # Reusable skills
├── templates/                # Task templates
├── docs/                     # Documentation
├── CLAUDE.md Reference/      # Reference files
│
├── history.jsonl             # Conversation history
├── plans/                    # Session plans
├── todos/                    # Todo states
├── projects/                 # Per-project state
├── plugins/                  # Downloaded plugins
│
├── debug/                    # ◆ Deletable cruft
├── downloads/                # ◆ Deletable cruft
├── file-history/             # ◆ Deletable cruft
├── shell-snapshots/          # ◆ Deletable cruft
├── statsig/                  # ◆ Deletable cruft
├── stats-cache.json          # ◆ Deletable cruft
├── telemetry/                # ◆ Deletable cruft
├── ide/                      # ◆ Deletable cruft
└── sqlite_mcp_server.db      # ◆ Deletable cruft
```

**◆ Deletable cruft** = Safe to delete anytime. Just wastes space. Regenerated as needed.

---

## After Copying

| What | Happens |
|------|---------|
| `.claude.json` | Copy to parent, then login regenerates OAuth tokens |
| `projects/` | Old paths ignored, new ones created as you work |
| Everything else | Just works |

---

## MCP Server Management

MCP servers are **disabled by default** to save context tokens (~34k+ with all 5 servers).

### Available Servers

| Server | Purpose | Tokens |
|--------|---------|--------|
| `playwright` | Browser automation, screenshots, cross-browser testing | ~14,446 |
| `filesystem` | File operations beyond Claude's built-in tools | ~9,220 |
| `memory` | Persistent knowledge graph across sessions | ~5,795 |
| `sqlite` | SQLite database operations | ~3,437 |
| `sequential-thinking` | Structured multi-step reasoning | ~1,554 |
| `context7` | Up-to-date library/framework documentation | ~TBD |
| `chrome-devtools` | Chrome debugging, Core Web Vitals, performance profiling | ~18,000 |
| `puppeteer` | Simple browser automation, screenshots, PDFs | ~8,000 |
| `browserbase` | Cloud browser automation, anti-bot, scaling | ~12,000 |

### Browser Automation Quick Reference

| Need | Use |
|------|-----|
| Cross-browser testing | `playwright` |
| Performance profiling | `chrome-devtools` |
| Simple screenshots | `puppeteer` |
| Cloud scale / anti-bot | `browserbase` |

See [docs/MCP-SERVERS.md](docs/MCP-SERVERS.md) for detailed comparison.

### Enable a Server (Current Session)

Edit `settings.json` and remove the server from `disabledMcpjsonServers`:

```json
{
  "disabledMcpjsonServers": [
    "sequential-thinking",
    // "playwright",  <-- remove from list to enable
    "memory",
    "filesystem",
    "sqlite"
  ]
}
```

**Changes take effect immediately** - no restart needed.

### Disable a Server

Add the server name back to `disabledMcpjsonServers` in `settings.json`.

### Quick Commands

```bash
# Check server status
/mcp

# View which servers are disabled
# Look at settings.json "disabledMcpjsonServers" array
```

### Configuration Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.mcp.json` | Server definitions (what servers exist) |
| `settings.json` | `disabledMcpjsonServers` array (which are disabled) |

### Workflow

1. Start session with all servers disabled (default)
2. Determine which server you need
3. Edit `settings.json` to remove that server from `disabledMcpjsonServers`
4. Server becomes available immediately
5. When done, add server back to disabled list (optional)

### Troubleshooting

- **`claude doctor` shows "1 MCP server failed"**: Often a transient timing issue. Check `/mcp` for actual status.
- **Server not connecting**: Set `MCP_TIMEOUT=10000` environment variable for slow-starting servers.
- **High token usage**: Re-disable servers you're not actively using.

---

## Platform Note

Windows MCP servers need `cmd /c` wrapper (already configured in `.mcp.json`).

For Linux/Mac, edit `.mcp.json` and change:
```json
"command": "cmd", "args": ["/c", "npx", ...]
```
To:
```json
"command": "npx", "args": [...]
```

---

## Cleanup (Optional)

Delete cruft to save space:
```powershell
rm -rf debug downloads file-history shell-snapshots statsig telemetry ide
rm stats-cache.json sqlite_mcp_server.db
```

---
description: Execute prompt(s) from ./prompts/ with automatic archiving to .archive/completed-prompts/
arguments:
  - name: prompt_ids
    description: "Prompt number(s) or name(s) to run. Examples: '001', '5 6 7', 'user-auth'. Use --parallel or --sequential for multiple prompts."
    required: false
---

# Run Prompt with Auto-Archive

Execute one or more prompts from `./prompts/` directory, then automatically archive completed prompts to `.archive/completed-prompts/` per CLAUDE.md session protocol.

## Workflow

1. Delegate to `/taches-cc-resources:run-prompt` to execute the prompt(s)
2. After successful completion, move prompt(s) from `./prompts/completed/` to `.archive/completed-prompts/`
3. Clean up any temporary directories

## Implementation

**Step 1:** Execute the tache run-prompt command with all arguments passed through

```
/taches-cc-resources:run-prompt {{prompt_ids}}
```

**Step 2:** After completion, check if `./prompts/completed/` exists and move contents to archive

```bash
# If prompts/completed/ exists, move all files to archive and cleanup
if [ -d "./prompts/completed" ]; then
  mkdir -p ".archive/completed-prompts"
  mv ./prompts/completed/* .archive/completed-prompts/ 2>/dev/null || true
  rmdir ./prompts/completed 2>/dev/null || true
fi
```

**Step 3:** Report final location of archived prompts

## Notes

- This wrapper ensures CLAUDE.md compliance (prompts archived to `.archive/completed-prompts/`)
- Transparent to user - just use `/run [prompt-id]` instead of `/run-prompt`
- Supports all `/run-prompt` features: single, parallel, sequential execution

---
description: Intelligent task router - analyzes your request and automatically delegates to optimal workflow (plan/prompt/meta-prompt/direct execution)
arguments:
  - name: task_description
    description: "Describe what you want to accomplish. Can be empty to check todos, or a full task description."
    required: false
---

# Start Task - Intelligent Workflow Router

Analyzes task and routes to optimal execution strategy.

## Routing Logic

**No task provided:** → `/check-todos`

**Task provided - analyze and route:**

| Keywords/Signals | Route To |
|-----------------|----------|
| "plan", "design", "architecture", "multi-phase" | `/create-plan {{task}}` |
| "should we", "which", "evaluate", "compare" | `/consider:[framework] {{task}}` |
| "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "race condition" | `Skill(debug-like-expert)` |
| Moderate complexity (30min-2hr, 3-10 files) | `/create-prompt {{task}}` → `/run` |
| "research then", "explore then", multi-stage | `/create-meta-prompt {{task}}` |
| Simple (1-2 files, <30min, clear scope) | Execute directly |

## Decision Framework Selection

| Decision Type | Framework |
|---------------|-----------|
| Technical choice | `/consider:first-principles` |
| Priority/urgency | `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` |
| Consequences | `/consider:second-order` |
| Root cause | `/consider:5-whys` |

## Examples

```bash
/start-task Plan the auth system                    # → /create-plan
/start-task Should we use WebSockets or SSE?       # → /consider:first-principles
/start-task Debug intermittent widget failure      # → debug-like-expert
/start-task Refactor calendar components           # → /create-prompt
/start-task Research GraphQL then implement        # → /create-meta-prompt
/start-task Fix typo in README                     # → direct execution
/start-task                                        # → /check-todos
```

# .claude/ Directory

Copy this entire folder anywhere. It's self-contained.

---

## Setup on New Machine

```powershell
# 1. Copy folder
cp -r ~/.claude /new/location/.claude

# 2. Copy config to parent (Claude Code expects it there)
cp /new/location/.claude/.claude.json /new/location/.claude.json

# 3. Login
claude
```

---

content.content:
1→# Claude Code Skills Library
     2→
     3→Custom skills for Claude Code - reusable across any project.
     4→
     5→**Version:** 2.0 (Merged & Generic)
     6→**Last Updated:** December 2025
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Generic Skills (Use for Any Project)
    11→
    12→### Development Skills
    13→
    14→| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
    15→|-------|---------|-------------|
    16→| **generic-code-reviewer** | Multi-stack code review | Before commits, PR reviews |
    17→| **generic-feature-developer** | Architecture patterns | Adding features, planning changes |
    18→| **generic-design-system** | Design tokens & patterns | UI implementation, styling |
    19→| **generic-ux-designer** | UX best practices | Design reviews, user flows |
    20→
    21→### Utility Skills
    22→
    23→| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
    24→|-------|---------|-------------|
    25→| **codebase-documenter** | Generate documentation | README, API docs, comments |
    26→| **tech-debt-analyzer** | Code health analysis | Audits, refactoring planning |
    27→| **test-specialist** | Testing guidance | Writing tests, debugging |
    28→| **frontend-enhancer** | Frontend patterns | UI improvements |
    29→| **document-skills** | Office docs (docx, xlsx, pptx, pdf) | Document creation |
    30→| **seo-analytics-auditor** | SEO analysis | Performance audits |
    31→
    32→---
    33→
    34→## Example Skills (Customization Templates)
    35→
    36→These skills demonstrate how to customize for specific project types. Copy and modify for your own projects.
    37→
    38→### example-static-site-* (Static HTML/CSS/JS Sites)
    39→- **example-static-site-code-reviewer** - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, performance focus
    40→- **example-static-site-design-system** - Minimalist design patterns
    41→- **example-static-site-feature-developer** - Landing page patterns
    42→- **example-static-site-ux-designer** - Lightweight UX constraints
    43→
    44→### example-react-app-* (React/TypeScript Applications)
    45→- **example-react-app-code-reviewer** - React/TypeScript, privacy-first
    46→- **example-react-app-design-system** - Dark mode, accessibility focus
    47→- **example-react-app-feature-developer** - Local-first architecture
    48→- **example-react-app-ux-designer** - Productivity app UX
    49→
    50→### example-fullstack-* (Full-Stack Next.js/NestJS)
    51→- **example-fullstack-code-reviewer** - Next.js + NestJS, Prisma
    52→- **example-fullstack-design-system** - Modern theme patterns
    53→- **example-fullstack-feature-developer** - Full-stack patterns
    54→- **example-fullstack-ux-designer** - SaaS app UX
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→## How to Use Skills
    59→
    60→### Automatic Activation
    61→Skills activate when relevant context appears in conversation.
    62→
    63→### Explicit Invocation
    64→Request skills directly:
    65→```
    66→Use generic-code-reviewer to review this code
    67→Use generic-design-system for the color choices
    68→Use tech-debt-analyzer on this codebase
    69→```
    70→
    71→### Skill Tool
    72→```
    73→Skill(generic-code-reviewer)
    74→Skill(tech-debt-analyzer)
    75→```
    76→
    77→---
    78→
    79→## Skill Structure
    80→
    81→Each skill follows this structure:
    82→
    83→```
    84→skills/
    85→└── [skill-name]/
    86→    ├── SKILL.md          # Main skill file (required)
    87→    ├── references/       # Reference materials (optional)
    88→    ├── assets/           # Templates, examples (optional)
    89→    └── scripts/          # Helper scripts (optional)
    90→```
    91→
    92→### SKILL.md Format
    93→
    94→```yaml
    95→---
    96→name: skill-name
    97→description: Brief description for when Claude should use this skill
    98→---
    99→
   100→# Skill Title
   101→
   102→Content with guidance, rules, patterns, examples...
   103→```
   104→
   105→---
   106→
   107→## Creating New Skills
   108→
   109→### 1. Create Folder
   110→```bash
   111→mkdir -p .claude/skills/[skill-name]
   112→```
   113→
   114→### 2. Create SKILL.md
   115→- YAML frontmatter with name and description
   116→- Comprehensive content for Claude to follow
   117→- Examples and patterns
   118→
   119→### 3. Add References (Optional)
   120→Place in `references/` folder for additional context.
   121→
   122→### 4. Test
   123→Verify skill activates when expected context appears.
   124→
   125→---
   126→
   127→## Skill Categories
   128→
   129→### By Tech Stack
   130→
   131→| Stack | Recommended Skills |
   132→|-------|-------------------|
   133→| HTML/CSS/JS | generic-*, example-static-site-* |
   134→| React/TypeScript | generic-*, example-react-app-* |
   135→| Next.js/NestJS | generic-*, example-fullstack-* |
   136→| Python | generic-code-reviewer, tech-debt-analyzer |
   137→| Any | codebase-documenter, test-specialist |
   138→
   139→### By Task Type
   140→
   141→| Task | Recommended Skills |

# Claude Code Skills Library

Custom skills for Claude Code - reusable across any project.

**Version:** 2.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025

---

## Generic Skills (Use for Any Project)

### Development Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **generic-code-reviewer** | Multi-stack code review | Before commits, PR reviews |
| **generic-feature-developer** | Architecture patterns | Adding features, planning changes |
| **generic-design-system** | Design tokens & patterns | UI implementation, styling |
| **generic-ux-designer** | UX best practices | Design reviews, user flows |

### Utility Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **codebase-documenter** | Generate documentation | README, API docs, comments |
| **tech-debt-analyzer** | Code health analysis | Audits, refactoring planning |
| **test-specialist** | Testing guidance | Writing tests, debugging |
| **frontend-enhancer** | Frontend patterns | UI improvements |
| **document-skills** | Office docs (docx, xlsx, pptx, pdf) | Document creation |
| **seo-analytics-auditor** | SEO analysis | Performance audits |

---

## Example Skills (Customization Templates)

These skills demonstrate how to customize for specific project types. Copy and modify for your own projects.

### example-static-site-* (Static HTML/CSS/JS Sites)
- **example-static-site-code-reviewer** - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, performance focus
- **example-static-site-design-system** - Minimalist design patterns
- **example-static-site-feature-developer** - Landing page patterns
- **example-static-site-ux-designer** - Lightweight UX constraints

### example-react-app-* (React/TypeScript Applications)
- **example-react-app-code-reviewer** - React/TypeScript, privacy-first
- **example-react-app-design-system** - Dark mode, accessibility focus
- **example-react-app-feature-developer** - Local-first architecture
- **example-react-app-ux-designer** - Productivity app UX

### example-fullstack-* (Full-Stack Next.js/NestJS)
- **example-fullstack-code-reviewer** - Next.js + NestJS, Prisma
- **example-fullstack-design-system** - Modern theme patterns
- **example-fullstack-feature-developer** - Full-stack patterns
- **example-fullstack-ux-designer** - SaaS app UX

---

## How to Use Skills

### Automatic Activation
Skills activate when relevant context appears in conversation.

### Explicit Invocation
Request skills directly:
```
Use generic-code-reviewer to review this code
Use generic-design-system for the color choices
Use tech-debt-analyzer on this codebase
```

### Skill Tool
```
Skill(generic-code-reviewer)
Skill(tech-debt-analyzer)
```

---

## Skill Structure

Each skill follows this structure:

```
skills/
└── [skill-name]/
    ├── SKILL.md          # Main skill file (required)
    ├── references/       # Reference materials (optional)
    ├── assets/           # Templates, examples (optional)
    └── scripts/          # Helper scripts (optional)
```

### SKILL.md Format

```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Brief description for when Claude should use this skill
---

# Skill Title

Content with guidance, rules, patterns, examples...
```

---

## Creating New Skills

### 1. Create Folder
```bash
mkdir -p .claude/skills/[skill-name]
```

### 2. Create SKILL.md
- YAML frontmatter with name and description
- Comprehensive content for Claude to follow
- Examples and patterns

### 3. Add References (Optional)
Place in `references/` folder for additional context.

### 4. Test
Verify skill activates when expected context appears.

---

## Skill Categories

### By Tech Stack

| Stack | Recommended Skills |
|-------|-------------------|
| HTML/CSS/JS | generic-*, example-static-site-* |
| React/TypeScript | generic-*, example-react-app-* |
| Next.js/NestJS | generic-*, example-fullstack-* |
| Python | generic-code-reviewer, tech-debt-analyzer |
| Any | codebase-documenter, test-specialist |

### By Task Type

| Task | Recommended Skills |
|------|-------------------|
| Code review | generic-code-reviewer, [project]-code-reviewer |
| New feature | generic-feature-developer |
| UI/Styling | generic-design-system |
| UX design | generic-ux-designer |
| Documentation | codebase-documenter |
| Code health | tech-debt-analyzer |
| Testing | test-specialist |
| Documents | document-skills |

---

## Skills vs Other Claude Code Features

| Feature | Use For |
|---------|---------|
| **Skills** | Domain knowledge, guidelines, patterns |
| **Slash Commands** | Quick actions, workflows |
| **Hooks** | Automated responses to events |
| **Subagents** | Complex multi-step tasks |
| **MCP Servers** | External tool integration |

---

## Best Practices

### When to Create a Skill
- Repeated guidance needed
- Project-specific standards
- Complex domain knowledge
- Reusable patterns

### When NOT to Create a Skill
- One-time instructions
- Simple preferences (use CLAUDE.md)
- External tool integration (use MCP)

### Skill Content Tips
- Be comprehensive but focused
- Include concrete examples
- Reference other skills when relevant
- Keep updated as project evolves

---

## See Also

- `.claude/automation-guide.md` - Workflow automation
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Development checklist
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom slash commands
- `.claude/templates/` - Task templates
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules

---

## Questions?

- Skills not activating? Check skill name and description match context
- Need new skill? Follow existing patterns in this folder
- Skill conflicts? Review descriptions for proper use cases

# CLAUDE.md Compliance Checklist

**Version:** 2.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Purpose:** Ensure every development task follows the mandatory CLAUDE.md workflow
**Usage:** Reference this checklist BEFORE starting ANY coding task

---

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules and core philosophy)
- [ ] **Sync with remote branch:**
  ```bash
  # For main branch
  git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
  # OR for master branch
  git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
  ```
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template from `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible**
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes
- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles**

---

## Project-Type Specific Checklists

### For UI/Visual Changes

- [ ] **Screenshot current state** before making changes
- [ ] **Make ONE change** at a time (not multiple)
- [ ] **Test in browsers:** Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] **Test responsiveness:** 375px, 768px, 1024px+
- [ ] **Compare to baseline** (visual diff)
- [ ] **Check animations work** (60fps target)
- [ ] **Get user approval** before committing

### For Node.js/React/TypeScript Projects

# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools - works with ANY project

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
9. [Project-Specific Configurations](#project-specific-configurations)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:

- **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

### Supported Project Types

This guide supports multiple project configurations:

| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
|--------------|------------|-------------|
| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |

---

### Before Starting ANY Task

1. **Sync with main branch:**
   ```bash
   # For main branch
   git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit

   # For master branch
   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
   ```

2. **Check workflow checklist:**

1. **Modular Architecture:** Separate skill, command, hook, and plugin systems allow fine-grained customization without bloating core config

2. **Context Token Management:** 11 MCP servers available but all disabled by default to minimize token usage (~34K+ overhead when all enabled)

3. **Permission Model:** Whitelist-based with 50+ specific bash patterns, git operations, and web domains - very granular control

4. **Multi-Project Support:** Tracks 15+ separate projects with per-project state and CLAUDE.md variants

5. **Workflow Automation:** Custom `/run` and `/start-task` commands automate the CLAUDE.md workflow with intelligent routing

6. **Comprehensive Skills:** 26 custom skills covering code review, design systems, testing, documentation, tech debt, and frontend enhancements across all major tech stacks

7. **Plugin-Based Extensibility:** 3 marketplaces provide 11+ plugins with specialized agents for PR reviews, feature dev, and commit automation

8. **Documentation-First:** Extensive documentation (8 docs files, 26 skills, custom templates) ensures consistent patterns across projects
